title: [Basculin] Battle Guide
summary: A 98 Speed glass cannon whose [adaptability] [wave-crash] hits with an effective base power near 240.
strengths: 98 Speed outruns almost every bulky threat in the tier
[flip-turn] keeps the [pivot] chain alive while chipping
weaknesses: 65/55 physical and special bulk is paper thin
[wave-crash] 33 percent re[coil] limits it to two or three c[lick]s

recommendedMoves.reason: Main [Water] STAB that with [adaptability] hits like effective base 240
[adaptability] priority that finishes fast [Electric] and [Ice] threats

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[Basculin]'s stat line isn't special, but its ability line is. [adaptability] replaces the usual 1.5x STAB multiplier with a flat 2.0x, which turns [wave-crash] into something closer to an effective base 240 attack. Base 98 Speed lets it outrun most of the bulky attackers in the tier, so [Basculin]'s job is to come in, click [Water] STAB twice, and leave before anything retaliates.

## Strengths
The [adaptability] multiplier turns even the [aqua-jet] priority slot into a real threat — [Electric] and [Ice] attackers that normally [revenge] [Water]-types lose to a boosted priority hit. [wave-crash] at 120 base power and 2.0x STAB is the strongest single-click [Water] attack in the format without needing setup or weather. [flip-turn] keeps the momentum chain going, and because it carries the same [adaptability] boost, the [pivot] damage is meaningful chip instead of a throwaway click.

## Weaknesses & Answers
[Basculin] has 70/65/55 bulk, which is a step below acceptable — anything resisted or neutral coming back at it usually KOs. [Grass] and [Electric] attackers both hit for 2x and they're everywhere in the format. Priority users like Conkeldurr [mach-punch] or [Fighting] [sucker-punch] pivots [revenge] it before [aqua-jet] comes online. And [wave-crash] recoil chews 33 percent HP per click, so two hits is usually the hard cap before [stealth-rock] damage forces a retreat.

## Recommended Moveset
[wave-crash] is the locked STAB and the reason to play [Basculin] at all. [aqua-jet] is non-negotiable as the priority finisher against [revenge] killers. [flip-turn] is the mandatory [pivot] slot and the only reason [Basculin] doesn't feel like a Choice-locked mon — it keeps the Speed chain alive while chipping with STAB damage. [crunch] is the coverage slot for bulky [psychic] and [Ghost] walls that would otherwise sit on [Water] STAB forever. [head-smash] is a possible alternative when extra pressure on [Ice] walls is needed, but the stacked recoil with [wave-crash] rarely survives two clicks.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[mystic-water] is the primary item because the additional 10 percent [Water] boost multiplies directly into the [adaptability] math. [choice-scarf] is the alternative when [tailwind] support isn't available and [Basculin] needs to outpace scarf Hydreigon tier attackers. [sitrus-berry] is the sustain pick to offset [wave-crash] recoil. The ability must be [adaptability] — reckless scales the recoil alongside the damage, and [mold-breaker] is a fringe pick. Tera [Water] stacks another 1.5x on top of everything for one massive click; Tera [Ghost] is the defensive pick that deletes the [Grass] and [Electric] weaknesses, making it a genuine [pivot] Tera.

## Teammates & Synergy
[Basculin] is a glass cannon and needs defensive pivots behind it — a [Ground] tank to [absorb] [Electric] hits and a [Dragon] or [Grass] [pivot] to eat [Grass] pressure. A [tailwind] setter covers the Speed axis without spending the [choice-scarf] slot. Intimidate support reduces the risk of priority [revenge] kills, and a [Fairy] or [Fighting] partner puts pressure on the [Dark] walls that sit on [crunch]. Fast cleaners behind [Basculin] snowball on the chip damage it generates through [flip-turn].

## Play Patterns
[Basculin] is a two-click pokemon, not a long-term answer. Bring it in on a forced switch during the mid-game, fire [wave-crash] into whatever is weak to [Water], then [flip-turn] out before recoil plus rocks removes it. Save [aqua-jet] for the precise moment a faster threat drops into KO range — one wasted priority click is usually one too many. Tera [Water] only when one massive hit ends the game; Tera [Ghost] earlier if the matchup is full of priority and [Basculin] needs to be the [pivot] spine.
